summary: Fix sync pagination so v2 endpoints use integer ?page=N and v3 endpoints use cursor pagination, instead of one cursor-shaped default that silently truncates v2 lists with >100 records.
reason: Greptile P1 finding on PR #433. ClickUp v2 (e.g. /v2/list/{id}/task) uses ?page=0,1,2 pagination starting at 0 and never returns a cursor field. The original `determinePaginationDefaults()` returned `cursorParam: cursor` universally; the loop's break condition `nextCursor == ''` always fired after the first v2 page (no cursor ever returned), so any list with more than 100 tasks was silently truncated. Per-resource pagination strategy synthesizes nextCursor from page-counter math when style is `page`, leaving cursor-style behavior unchanged for v3 doc/chat. Verified end-to-end via --dry-run: task sends `?page=0&limit=100`, channel sends `?limit=100&with_message_since=...` (no page).
